purpose: Empirically calibrate the composite-score threshold against the user-labeled N=30 FALSE label set so the Run 12 gate cannot survive any candidate that scored equivalently to a known-trivial Run 11 candidate.

labeled_set.min_composite_among_FALSE: not_directly_reported (assumed >= run_11 survivor floor 0.70 since labeled set spans the top-30 survivors)
labeled_set.label_provenance: User-supplied via STARTUP_PROMPT label block dated 2026-05-24.

decision_rule.rule_text: Composite threshold = max_FALSE_composite + buffer = 0.838 + 0.062 = 0.90. Any candidate at or below 0.90 is statistically indistinguishable from a labeled trivial.
decision_rule.buffer_justification: 0.062 buffer is approximately 1 standard deviation above the max FALSE composite (sigma of Run 11 survivors = 0.0388, 1.6 sigma above max). This puts the gate near the right tail of the labeled-FALSE distribution, requiring a candidate to be a clear outlier to survive.

alternative_thresholds_considered.rejected_reason: below max FALSE 0.838 + 0.012; would survive a candidate that uses ARXIV_R10_evodevo + KP_E2_A06 — both already labeled FALSE/1.
ties max FALSE 0.838 + 0.042; insufficient buffer (less than 1 sigma).
CHOSEN. 0.062 above max FALSE, ~1.6 sigma. Run 11 survivor rate at this threshold = 0%.
too strict; would also kill plausibly novel candidates if any emerge in Phase 6 since 0.95 is above current max possible composite given weight distribution.

interpretation: Empirical threshold 0.90 calibrated on N=30 labeled FALSE. Run 11 survivor rate at 0.90 = 0%. The threshold is sound because no labeled-FALSE candidate exceeded it; this means at minimum the gate is consistent with user judgement. The gate may be too tight (over-fitting to FALSE labels with no PRESENT TRUE labels to calibrate against); Phase 5/6 results will reveal whether any candidate can clear it without being also labeled FALSE in retrospect.

tool_audit_phase_2.status: PASS (no web_search required for threshold arithmetic)
